welcome: Welcome to the music theory app!

footer.copyright: © 2026 Music Theory App. All rights reserved.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The page you are looking for does not exist or may have been moved.

error.title: Something went wrong
error.description: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.

pianoRange.title: Piano Range
pianoRange.description: Select the range of piano keys you want to use for interval training. The larger the range, the more diverse the intervals you'll hear!

intervals.title: Intervals to Train
intervals.description: Choose which intervals you want to practice. The more intervals you select, the more varied your training will be!

answerMode.title: Answer Mode
answerMode.description: How do you want to answer each question? Different modes will help you develop different skills. Try them all to find your favourite way to train!

keyboard.label: Play It
keyboard.desc: Click the top note on a piano keyboard

sheet.label: Write It
sheet.desc: Place the interval on a musical staff

playbackModes.title: Playback Mode
playbackModes.description: Choose whether the intervals are played melodically (one after the other) or harmonically (at the same time). Melodic intervals help you recognize the individual sound of each note, while harmonic intervals train you to hear how the notes blend together!

intervals.title: Interval Ear Training
intervals.description: Train your ear with different intervals. Get a feel for each interval and improve your recognition skills. Get instant feedback to track your progress and master interval recognition!

question.title: What interval do you hear?
question.description: Listen carefully, then submit your answer below.

keyboardAnswer.title: Click the top note
keyboardAnswer.description: The <blue>blue</blue> key is the bottom note. Click the key that completes the interval.

sheetAnswer.title: Place the top note on the staff
sheetAnswer.description: Select an accidental if needed, then click the staff to place the note.

feedbackPhase.youAnswered: You answered {answered} — the correct answer was {correct}.
feedbackPhase.enharmonicIntervals: These intervals are enharmonic, meaning they sound the same but have different names and are notated differently.
feedbackPhase.redundantNatural: {note} doesn't need a natural sign — it's natural by default.
feedbackPhase.redundantKeyAccidental: The accidental on {note} is already implied by the key signature.

notInScale.major: {note} is not part of the {tonic} major scale.
notInScale.harmonicMinor: {note} is not part of the harmonic {tonic} minor scale.

chords.title: Chord Ear Training
chords.description: Train your ear to recognise chords — triads, seventh chords, and their inversions. Get a feel for each chord and improve your recognition skills. Get instant feedback to track your progress and master chord recognition!

question.title: What chord do you hear?
question.description: Listen carefully, then submit your answer below.

keyboardAnswer.title: Click the remaining notes
keyboardAnswer.description: The <blue>blue</blue> key is the bass note. Click the remaining notes to complete the chord.

sheetAnswer.title: Place the remaining notes on the staff
sheetAnswer.description: Select an accidental if needed, then click the staff to place each note.

feedbackPhase.youAnswered: You answered {answered} — the correct answer was {correct}.
